summary: QA > Bulk update > Nutrients Bulk Review > Multiple observations
description:
ISSUE - 1

When clicking on a missing check box on Cholesterol, Sodium, we are adding Missing labels to both fat and carbs? They are not related to those at all.

[image: image-20260209-182911-20260210-060158.png]

[image: image-20260209-183046-20260210-060201.png]

However, If move the missing check box to trans fat, then the label is corrected for the Fat and is still appearing on the carbs.

ISSUE - 2

Notice on the above screen shot, how we have the (M) in bold, we should be doing this when the user clicks the box as well and also be consistent with all labeling.

[image: image-20260209-183203-20260210-060255.png]

[image: image-20260209-183141-20260210-060252.png]

ISSUE - 3

Giving '0' count to the total fat automatically the counts will add in the *Sat. Fat (g)** & Trans Fat (g)

ISSUE - 4

When a serving size picked on an item that has multiple serving sizes, why are we not adding –- for the columns like we are doing for items with one serving size. We should not be treating them any different as long as a serving size is selected. We are actually doing this for even items without a serving size selected so I am not sure what the logic is here.

[image: image-20260206-201352.png]

 

ISSUE - 5

When selecting only Cholesterol for example as missing and nothing else, why are we all of a sudden flagging other values as missing? Also why are we using a orange color for missing instead of the red especially since these are mandatory values. Compare the color on the bean and cheese burrito vs the Buffalo Ranch Chicken Sticks for this example. Also once any value has been added for a particular item, we should be flagging the item as now missing mandatory/non mandatory information.

[image: image-20260206-202209.png]

 

ISSUE - 6

We need to have proper spacing between the commas in the Fat and Carbs columns with the new combined values. We do not seem to be doing this correctly.

[image: image-20260206-202413.png]

 

ISSUE -7

Why are we not displaying the Non Mandatory Alert for the users when non mandatory information is missing? Seems were only showing it on the view nutrients section but this is key on both sections.

[image: image-20260206-202941.png]

ISSUE - 8

We have the Display the (M) in bold in the view vitamins tab

summary: Item onboarding - pack size filters - PO observations
description:
Firstly, obviously there should be a way to deselect from the dropdown without using the Clear button. This is simply a single select dropdown; there should still be the X to clear the selection.

More importantly, I don’t know how we’re defining several of these flags.

This is a new session I have just started populating. In the video below I have not made any changes to the Pack Size section.

[image: 20251117-2217-16.2525841.mp4]

This would obviously mean several things:

- 6 out of 7 items are missing Pack-Serving Link. All items except the Non-Food item (Bleach) are missing this relationship. Yet when I select this option, 3 items are filtered. I’m not sure what we consider this link to be, but it’s not correct.
- Base Unit Weight should show only the items that require a weight but cannot be linked via Sub-Qty

[image: image-20251117-223312.png]

In other words, BU Weight is a subset of Pack-Serving Link. Pack-Serving Link is ALL items missing the pack-serving relationship. BU Weight is the items that can ONLY be provided by Weight. If we look at the Serving Info, we can see several of these items are eligible to be linked via sub-qty (1 Each, 1 Tortilla, 1 Bowl). The other items (1/2 Cup, 1 Ounce) MUST be linked via weight. These are the items we’re expecting to see when we use this filter.

[image: image-20251117-223327.png]

Additionally, this entire filter card section needs to be properly formatted, styled and sized. It’s not the right size, has no visible borders, etc. These issues are present on other sections as well. Please compare to Ingredient Info for comparison to proper styling, etc.

[image: image-20251117-223938.png]

summary: IM - Recipe Nutrition Report - need Percent of calories from Macronutrients
description:
The Recipe Nutrition Report already exists, but per the USDA Requirements for software approval, is missing some specific data.

It is a requirement to display the Percent of Calories from the Macronutrients (Protein, Carbohydrate, Total Fat, and Saturated Fat.)

The percentage of calories from protein, carbohydrate, total fat, and saturated fat for the recipe should be calculated as follows:

Protein = 4 Calories per gram
Carbohydrate = 4 Calories per gram
Fat = 9 Calories per gram
Saturated Fat = 9 Calories per gram

Multiply the total grams of protein by four and divide by the total calories. This total equals the percentage of calories from protein.
Multiply the total grams of carbohydrate by four and divide by the total calories. This total equals the percentage of calories from carbohydrates.
Multiply the total grams of fat by nine and divide by the total calories. This total equals the percentage of calories from fat.
Multiplн the total grams of saturated fat by nine and divide by the total calories. This total equals the percentage of calories from saturated fat.

NOTE: The total of the first three percentages (protein, carbohydrate, and fat) should approximate but may not equal 100%

Example with calculations:

For the below recipe shown in the screenshot , recipe code: LR13126 

Total fat= 17.45grams ===> 17.45*_9 = 157.05 kcal ===> (100*_157.05)/258 = 60.87% Percent of calories. (Simialr calculation for Saturatedfat as well. )
Protein= 25.11 grams ===> 25.11*_4  = 100.44 kcal ===> (100*_100.44)/258 = 38.93% Percent of calories. (Simialr calculation for Carbohydrates and total sugars. )

THIS IS A GRAPG TO BE USED AS REFERENCE TO HOW THE MUCKUP WILL LOOK LIKE

[image: image-20210924-132121.png]

[image: image-20210917-194936.png]
acceptance_criteria: The Recipe Nutrition Report will contain percentages for the macronutrients (protein, carbohydrate, total fat, and saturated fat.)

summary: IM - Item Onboarding - Column headers and data fields for Excel Import/Export
description: As a district manager trying to use the onboarding tool, sometimes it would be better for me to work in the excel version as I am more comfortable working through an excel format workflow. acceptance_criteria:
- On Initial Info, Item Type (Food/Non Food) should have selections of (Food or Non Food) and is mandatory
** Format the cells to be greyed out that are not needed for Non Food Items (Nutrients, Components, Ingredient Info, Allergens)
- “# of Inventory Units” (Currently, % of Inventory Units) should only appear on the excel if user has enabled the “Pack Size Info” section and must be entered
** Default to None/Decide Later if section is not enabled
** Mandatory to answer if enabled
- “Is this a Direct Menu Item” should only appear if the user has enabled the “Menu Item Info” section and must be entered
** Default to None/Decide later if section is not enabled
** Mandatory to answer if enabled
- Keep Non-Fluid/ Fluid Item column and provide (Non-Fluid/Fluid) drop down and is a mandatory field to select
** IF Fluid is selected, provide Fluid Measure in Measure drop down (Milliliter, Fluid Ounce, Gallon)
** IF Non-Fluid is selected, provide Weight Measure (Gram, Ounce, Pound)
** Remove The conditional grey out formatting for fluid and non fluid
- Format Serving Size Quantity and Serving Size Weight to only be integer and decimal values
- Format Edible Yield Factor to be an integer and decimal values and should never exceed 100
- Format Nutrient values to only be integer and decimal values
- Food Components need to ONLY include 1 field for each component and or variety and just call it the name of the component or variety
** Columns should just be the name of the component or sub component
** Include Variety field as the last field for that component section with Yes/ No selections (Fruit Juice and Vegetable Juice
*** EX: Fruit, Fruit Juice OR [person], Non Whole Grain, [person]
*** For Milk, Include a “Milk Variety” column and the drop down values are the variety selection
*** For Grains, No need to have a grain component, provide a column for each sub grain
*** For Veg sub groups, provide column for each sub group and no need to have a “Vegetable” component column
- Remove Custom Allergens Column (User must use the UI to add custom allergens)
- Add Conditional formatting, if user marks None Indicated as Yes, grey out the allergen columns
- Remove all Asterisks as we are allowing partial data and verifying on the UI tool
- For Downloading Data from existing session,

*** EX: Non food item being exported with Edible Yield Factor, Locally Grown & Buy American and Components marked as no
- IF a value has not been entered, DO not export with 0 value and leave as blank
** EX: Serving Size Weight not entered on the UI but downloaded as 0

** Currently we are displaying No for every item regardless of food/non food

summary: IM- API - Remove Parenthetical serving measure information from CNDB Items
description:
USDA has requested that we remove Parenthetical serving measure information when information becomes inaccurate based on the change in unit of measure or quantity of measure.

Example: Item #1053 "1 cup (yields 2 cups whipped) when added to a recipe in a different volume than 1 cup, say 2 cups, it will still display (yields 2 cups whipped). This information is inaccurate and must be removed.

Side note:
Researching on Classic, determined that this removal is already happening on some items but not all. There is no way to pinpoint how this removal is being determined but could possibly be helpful if analyzed.

More notes and examples.

The requirement is for any CN item if it’s measure was given with some information in parenthesis , then clean it by removing it and import the measure.

Problem is for this item #1053 : one item measure is given as 1 cup, fluid (yields 2 cups whipped) = 238 grams. so, per USDA “yields 2 cups of Whipped” is valid only for 1 cup, fluid.

lets say, you used 10 cups , fluid in a recipe then showing that as 10 cup, fluid (yields 2 cups whipped) is incorrect. like its shown in the below screenshot. thats the problem we are trying to avoid by cleaning the measure description and importing it as 1 cup, fluid = 238 grams.

[image: image-20210916-194109 (31ebaaba-2a4d-4c4e-87ef-51c485fd4858).png]

Example 2: for this item #10130 item measure says 2 slices (6 per 6-oz pkg.) , but when clicked on possible measures it is showing 1 slice also 6 per 6oz pkg. which is wrong.

[image: image-20210916-195139 (2d452c76-fefc-496a-ba18-e394fd9f10e8).png]
acceptance_criteria:
Remove Parenthetical information from 1 cup (yields 2 cups whipped).

Classic import of this information displays correctly but no determination on how certain items are keeping parenthetical information and some are not.

summary: IM - Item Details - Food tab - Allow nutrient entry by percentage

- Iron - 18mg
- Calcium - 1300mg
- Vitamin A - 900mcg RAE
** we’ll need to change this from IU to mcg
** I think we can ignore the ‘RAE’
- Vitamin C - 90mg
- Vitamin D - 20mcg
** we’ll need to change this from IU to mcg
- Potassium - 4700mg
- Moisture - no daily value ?
- Ash - no daily value ?

- Keep the existing mg/mcg fields in the Per Serving column
** Implement logic which allows users to use either field
** one field will automatically update based on user input in the other field
- Make sure View and Edit are similar enough
** main point is showing some separation between macronutrients and vitamin nutrients
- In View mode, Cholesterol should not be indented under Total Fat
** it is its own separate nutrient
- Other Improvements
** Disable the ability to increase numbers by scrolling within a text field. This is a very dangerous area to allow this functionality
** Ensure it continues to be keyboard friendly

[image: image-20220830-145808.png]

Rough Mockup Example:

PrimeroEdge for example (see how the same column can be used for DRV% and % of Calories since we only need one or the other)

[image: NXT-33303 1.png]

[image: NXT-34511 1.png]

summary: IM - Item Import - Make Inventory Ready fields optional
description:
As a District Admin I want the ability to import items in a non-Inventory Ready state so I can import items into an incomplete state and finish the inventory readiness in the UI.

Example of an item being imported into Inventory Ineligible status (serving is not linked to base unit):

[image: image-20231031-142940.png]
acceptance_criteria:
- Item Import should allow for optional input of all Inventory Ready data points
** Item Category, Storage Category, Valuation Group
** Pack Size unit fields
** Fair Market Value
** pack-serving linkage
- Items should import into the UI in the appropriate Inventory Readiness status (Ineligible, Incomplete, etc.)
- Checks for Pack Size-serving size relationships for dual unit possibility
** If the serving size quantity is 1 AND the serving description is NOT a standard measure, perform the following checks:
**# If the serving size weight (in grams +/- 15%) equals the lowest unit weight/measure (in gram/ounces/pounds) and has the same unit/measure description, use the serving size weight and same description
**# If the serving size weight (in grams +/- 15%) equals the lowest unit weight/measure (in grams/ounces/pounds) but has a different unit/measure description, use the serving size weight and the serving size description.
**# If the serving size weight (in grams +/- 15%) does not equal the lowest unit weight/measure (in ounces or grams) and has the same unit/measure description, generate an error as duplicate serving size/lowest unit measure is not allowed, and do not import.
**# If the serving size weight (in grams +/- 15%) does not equal the lowest unit weight/measure (in ounces or grams) and has a different unit/measure description, import it as a pack unit and serving unit like normal.
** When converting between different units, we should account for rounding with a margin of +/- 15%. If the units are the same and weight is not within threshold, we should flag them as an error when the descriptions match; otherwise, treat them as normal. (Explaining point #3)
